name: grant-exceeds-envelope-critical
description: R15 ratification: a critical-tier tool with a covering grant whose native tierCap is "critical" (i.e. it genuinely covers) is clamped by the admin envelope's grantCeiling: "sensitive" — one rank below critical, so no grant this envelope could ever issue reaches critical. Unlike the sensitive-tier grant-exceeds-envelope case (grant-exceeds-envelope.json), there is NO fall-through to a layer-4 explicit_config_allow here: that path only ever exists at sensitive tier (L0ReasonCode.ExplicitConfigAllow is never emitted for critical), so critical is categorically unreachable by any grant under this envelope — approving would just mint an ephemeral grant the same ceiling would reject, a futile loop the engine avoids by denying decisively and loudly at layer 3 instead of escalating to pending_approval. Contrast with the no-grant baseline under the identical envelope (packages/core/src/precedence.test.ts), which instead falls through all the way to layer 4 and resolves pending_approval/no_grant_critical — proving the asymmetry is deliberate: an over-ceiling grant is diagnosed explicitly (grant_exceeds_envelope) rather than collapsing into the generic no-grant path.
